description: Plan-amendment proposal envelope. Chain-bearing (carries prev_envelope_hash) but NOT crypto-bearing — amendments inherit the cluster's trust context. Severity-correction (e.g. observed_failure → major) is consumer-side per prd.md:289 / ADR-010; the schema admits looser combinations so the discrepancy can be surfaced explicitly.

envelope_kind.description: Discriminator literal pinning the plan-amendment-request shape. Distinguishes from `plan_signoff` (FR-B9) and other envelope kinds.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version. Pinned to "8.6.0" for the cycle-005 ship line.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at amendment-request emission.

cluster_id.description: Stable cluster identifier (consumer-shaped). Identifies the cluster the amendment is scoped to.

actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the actor proposing the amendment (consumer-shaped).

parent_signoff_id.description: Identifier of the signoff this amendment is proposed against. Consumer-side resolution joins this to a PlanSignoffEnvelope record in their ledger.

parent_plan_hash.description: sha256:<64-hex> content hash of the plan being amended. FR-C4 may be invoked against this field if the consumer wants to validate the parent plan is still on file.

proposed_delta.description: Free-text amendment proposal. Capped at 64 KB code points; the canonical-JSON byte length is bounded by the cluster's envelope-cap policy (consumer-side). Format is consumer-shaped (markdown, structured diff, natural-language proposal); the schema only checks non-emptiness and the size cap.

severity.description: Two-member exhaustive severity enum for plan amendments. Severity-correction (e.g. forcing observed_failure → major) is consumer-side policy per prd.md:289 / ADR-010; the schema admits the looser combination.

trigger_class.description: Four-member exhaustive enum for what triggered the amendment. Schema enforces enum membership only; severity-correction mapping (e.g. observed_failure → major) is consumer-side.

rationale.description: Free-text rationale explaining why the amendment is needed. Capped at 8 KB code points.

recommended_paths.description: Non-empty array of recommended paths. An amendment MUST propose at least one concrete path; "do nothing" should be expressed explicitly as a path entry, not as an empty array.

items.description: A single recommended-path object. All three fields (id, summary, tradeoff) are required; no optional fields.

id.description: Stable identifier for this recommended path.

summary.description: Short summary of what this path proposes.

tradeoff.description: Explicit tradeoff statement — what this path costs or risks, so the deciding consumer can compare paths on a shared axis. minLength: 1 forces authors to state the tradeoff (even "none" is a tradeoff declaration); empty strings are rejected.

jury_recommendation.description: Jury recommendation text, or null if no jury was consulted. Consumer-shaped — the schema does not enforce length or format.

chain_refs.description: Chain reference for envelope-level continuity. Unlike PlanSignoffEnvelope, no parent_signoff_hash here — the amendment's lineage to a signoff is captured by parent_signoff_id at the top level.

prev_envelope_hash.description: sha256:<64-hex> hash of the previous envelope's canonical-JSON form. Genesis position uses the all-zero anchor.
